title: The Horse and the Stag
lines: A Horse and a Stag were contending for the same pasture.
'You cannot stay here,' said the Horse.
'Perhaps," replied the Stag, 'but my horns are sharper than your hooves.'
They fought bitterly, neither willing to give way.
At last they both fled when a Lion appeared, ready to eat them both.
'We were foolish to waste our strength on each other," said the Horse.
'We should have been watching for our real enemy.'
moral: Enemies who fight among themselves are easy prey for a greater foe.
